per_turn_leaks.student_message: I need a precise description. Where exactly is the problem and when did it start?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: The pain started about three weeks ago, and at first, it was just this dull ache that I could tolerate. But over time, it's gotten worse – sharper, more crampy, and it feels like it's radiating from my lower left abdomen, kind of near the appendix scar I have. The bloody stools started a week or so after the pain began, and they're always bright red blood mixed with stool. It's really been affecting my work performance because of the constant trips to the bathroom, and I'm getting pretty fatigued.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: In cases like yours, associated symptoms are critical. Are you experiencing any sweating, nausea, or other symptoms during episodes?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I get a little lightheaded when I stand up too quickly after using the bathroom, but no sweating or nausea specifically. Although, to be honest, my joints have been bothering me a bit – I've had achy pain in both knees and ankles for about two weeks now, which is weird because I'm not usually active enough to cause that kind of strain.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: I'm going to ask you some direct questions and I need straightforward answers. Any nausea, vomiting, or stomach upset?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: No, I don't have any nausea or vomiting. The only thing that's been really bothering me is this crampy abdominal pain and the bloody stools with mucus. Like I said, it's been pretty constant over the past week, especially since I've had to start using loperamide to try and manage the diarrhea, which hasn't been super effective so far.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Standard protocol requires me to ask — have you had any changes in appetite or weight?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: To be honest, I've been having a bit of trouble with my appetite lately. The diarrhea has made it hard for me to keep food down, and even when I do eat, I feel really full and uncomfortable afterwards. As for weight loss, I have lost about 4 kg (9 lbs) over the past three weeks, which is probably due to the lack of appetite and the constant trips to the bathroom.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Family history is essential for your assessment. Has anyone in your immediate family had serious medical conditions?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: My father has type 2 diabetes, but my mother is healthy. That's about it, doc. My sister did get diagnosed with Crohn's disease a few years ago when she was in her early thirties.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Before I finalize my assessment, is there absolutely anything else I should know? This is important.
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Doc, I think we've covered everything pretty thoroughly. To confirm, I'll reiterate that I've been experiencing worsening abdominal pain on my left side, which started about three weeks ago and has been getting sharper over time. The bloody stools with mucus started a week or so after the pain began, and they're always bright red blood mixed with stool. I've also had some joint pain in my knees and ankles that seems to be related to stress from work and possibly the abdominal issues. As for other symptoms, I get lightheaded when standing up quickly, but no sweating, nausea, or vomiting. My diet is irregular due to long work hours, and I've lost about 4 kg (9 lbs) over the past three weeks due to a lack of appetite and constant trips to the bathroom. I'm not sure if any of these symptoms are related, but I want to make sure we explore all possibilities to get me feeling better as soon as possible.
